title: 10 killed after massive fire breaks out at restaurant in south Delhi's Malviya Nagar
content: Tragedy struck Malviya Nagar this morning as a devastating fire at a restaurant claimed four lives. Fire services responded swiftly, rescuing eleven individuals who were promptly transported to the hospital. The cause of the blaze remains under investigation, with authorities working to uncover the circumstances surrounding this unfortunate incident.

title: TMC vs TMC out in open: Major setback for Mamata as rebel MLAs claim support of nearly 50 MLAs
content: On May 9, Trinamool Congress national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee wrote to the assembly speaker proposing senior party leader Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay's name as the leader of opposition. TMC MLAs Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha, however, complained to the speaker alleging that "no resolution was adopted regarding the selection of the leader of opposition on May 6."

title: IND vs AFG: Siraj's availability hangs in the balance; Nabi on standby for one-off Test
content: Mohammed Siraj's availability for the Afghanistan Test remains uncertain, with selectors monitoring the pacer's workload after a long IPL season. Jammu & Kashmir seamer Auqib Nabi, one of the six net bowlers, is on standby and will join the squad if Siraj is rested. The one-off Test with Afghanistan gets underway on June 6.

title: US proposes action against 60 countries over forced labour concerns: Why India is on the list
content: The US Trade Representative has proposed additional tariffs on goods from 54 economies, including India, over concerns about forced labor in supply chains. This move, stemming from a Section 301 investigation, could impact India's exports, particularly those relying on Chinese inputs, despite existing domestic prohibitions on forced labor.

title: Uber sets AI usage limit for employees after exceeding AI budget
content: Uber has implemented monthly usage caps of $1,500 per AI coding tool for its employees due to exceeding its AI budget. This move aims to responsibly manage the rising costs associated with AI adoption and experimentation across the company.  The company previously reported that AI agents contributed to 10% of its code.

title: 'Can't breathe': Video shows UK cops arresting stabbed teen as Indian-origin attacker posed as victim
content: A stabbing in Southampton led to a debate on religious symbols after a Sikh man fatally attacked a teenager, falsely claiming racial abuse. While the attacker was jailed for life, the incident sparked calls to restrict kirpans, prompting government assurances on religious freedom and condemnation of divisive rhetoric. Police actions at the scene are under investigation.

title: Why is stock market down? BSE Sensex, Nifty50 crash 1% — top reasons for fall
content: Both the Sensex and Nifty declined more than 1% as investor sentiment weakened amid growing concerns over the Iran-US conflict, continued foreign institutional investor (FII) outflows and a host of other market challenges.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su: Companies do not need people who know how to use AI tools
content: AMD CEO Lisa Su advised graduates entering the AI-driven job market to prioritize purpose, judgment, and problem-solving over simply learning AI tools. She emphasized that employers need individuals who can determine AI's application, not just operate the technology. Su highlighted that human judgment remains crucial for deciding which problems are worth solving and taking responsibility for outcomes.

title: 2 suitcases stashed with termite-eaten cash, firearm found in Kolkata college
content: Termite-damaged currency notes were discovered in two suitcases within a locked storeroom at Surendranath College during a dengue-prevention clean-up drive. Police also found a firearm in another locked union room. The discovery has sparked a political blame game, with allegations of corruption against a former Trinamool functionary.

title: BJP shares AI video mocking DK Shivakumar ahead of Karnataka swearing-in
content: Ahead of DK Shivakumar's Karnataka CM oath, BJP launched a sharp attack, criticizing the Congress government's handling of waterlogging and alleging neglect of farmers and Dalits.  The party shared an AI-generated video and posts on X, accusing the new administration of governance failures and broken promises to key communities. Shivakumar is set to be sworn in today.

title: 'Ab sab chuk jayega': The IPL dream that will free Mukul Choudhary's family from debt
content: After a breakthrough IPL season with Lucknow Super Giants, Mukul Choudhary is set to fulfil a dream far bigger than cricket: helping his family clear a long-standing bank loan. From overnight bus journeys between Gurugram and Bharatpur to years of living in rented homes, the youngster's rise has been built on sacrifice, hard work and unwavering family support. Now, armed with lessons from Rishabh Pant, Mitchell Marsh and Nicholas Pooran, Mukul believes the IPL has changed his life and opened the road to bigger ambitions.

title: Microsoft announces one of the largest enterprise AI rollouts at Infosys, TCS and Wipro
content: India's IT giants Infosys, TCS, and Wipro have collectively deployed over 300,000 Microsoft 365 Copilot licenses, marking a rapid enterprise AI rollout. These companies are integrating AI into core workflows, moving beyond tool deployment to an AI-driven operating model. This significant adoption signals India's leadership in scaling agentic AI across Asia and globally.

title: More Trump tariffs? US names India in its Section 301 findings; proposes additional duties
content: The USTR released the outcome of 60 investigations conducted under Section 301, identifying India among 54 economies that, according to its assessment, do not have adequate measures in place to prohibit or effectively prevent the import of goods allegedly produced using forced labour.

title: Did Sooryavanshi deliver the greatest batting campaign in IPL history?
content: If the question is which batter produced the most extreme combination of volume, speed and power across a single IPL season, the evidence points overwhelmingly in one direction. The IPL has seen bigger run tallies. It has seen cleaner six-hitters. What it had never seen before 2026 was a player score nearly 800 runs while striking at 237 and smashing 72 sixes. For 18 seasons, volume and violence existed separately. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi merged them, and that is why his IPL campaign 2026 may be one of the greatest batting seasons ever.

title: Maharashtra-model in Bengal? Didi fights to fend off crisis amid MLA exodus buzz
content: The political landscape in Bengal is charged with speculation as a prominent BJP minister asserts that more than 50 Trinamool MLAs could defect amidst growing schisms within the party. This development comes on the heels of the 'Signgate' scandal, where ousted MLAs are claiming their signatures were forged for the Leader of Opposition position.

title: After CBSE & NTA, IIT Roorkee faces JEE Advanced 2026 data exposure claim
content: A cybersecurity disclosure has placed infrastructure linked to JEE Advanced 2026 under scrutiny after researcher Rylen Anil alleged that a cloud storage configuration exposed thousands of candidate-related records and admit-card PDFs. IIT Roorkee, the organising institute, acknowledged the configuration issue and said corrective action was being taken. The development comes amid recent cybersecurity concerns involving CBSE's On-Screen Marking system and NTA's re-examination portal, highlighting growing attention on data security across India's examination ecosystem.

title: 'Economic Fury' on Iran: US seizes Tehran's biggest crypto network amid peace talks
content: The US has imposed sanctions on Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, Nobitex, and its executives, accusing them of aiding the Iranian government and sanctioned entities in bypassing Western restrictions. This move, dubbed 'Economic Fury,' aims to increase economic pressure on Tehran amid ongoing conflicts.

title: 'AIADMK tie-up cost vote share': What 'sidelined' Annamalai told BJP top brass
content: Former BJP Tamil Nadu chief K Annamalai resigned, citing party sidelining and poor election results, including a dropped vote share. He met Amit Shah and Nitin Nabin, with sources divided on his decision to quit. Annamalai reportedly plans a new regional outfit, potentially drawing cadres from the BJP.

title: Quote of the day by Zendaya
content: Zendaya's insights in 'Between U and Me' resonate powerfully with tweens, as she passionately advocates for embracing one's individuality over blending in. By urging them to cultivate their unique abilities, she inspires a journey toward self-acceptance and inner happiness, empowering them to shine in their own light.

title: Watch: Air defence battle over Kuwait as Patriot missiles respond to Iranian strike
content: Middle East tensions flared as Iran launched missiles targeting US facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain, which were intercepted or failed to hit. The US responded with strikes on an Iranian military site. This escalation follows a major US military campaign against Iran earlier this year, with ongoing exchanges and stalled negotiations pushing the region towards wider conflict.

title: Racketeers log in again! Guaranteed scores offered in NEET re-exam for hefty price
content: New racketeers are reportedly selling 'NEET re-exam question papers' and 'guaranteed scores' online for hefty sums, ranging from Rs 60,000 to Rs 20 lakh. An activist has lodged a complaint with cybercrime police to investigate these social media channels, which claim to have leaked exam material and influential backing.  Authorities are urged to probe these fraudulent claims.

title: Explained in 10 charts: Why India fell behind Taiwan, S Korea in stock market ranks
content: At a time of global economic turmoil, why are some global markets like the US, Taiwan and South Korea rallying? Why has the Indian stock market slipped from record highs, giving negative returns over the last few quarters?

title: Iran targets US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait; America strikes Qeshm Island as ceasefire frays
content: Kuwait's air defense systems intercepted incoming missiles and drones, with loud explosions reported across the country. The military confirmed these were the result of air defense operations against hostile aerial targets. Citizens were cautioned against approaching any fallen debris, with authorities urging reliance on official information.

title: Is Mojtaba Khamenei alive? Rubio says Iran supreme leader 'increasingly engaging'
content: In a surprising announcement, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio revealed that Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei is not only alive but also taking a more hands-on approach in governance. This update comes after the February 28, 2026, attack in Tehran, which claimed the life of his father, former leader Ali Khamenei, among others.

title: How Mathura ashram became trap for women; ex-IITian sadhu held for sexual abuse
content: An IIT Roorkee graduate, posing as a spiritual guru in Mathura, has been arrested for allegedly luring and sexually exploiting young women. The accused, Abhishek Mishra, used online platforms to attract followers and coerced women into relationships under the guise of religious rituals. A complaint from a nursing student led to his arrest and the recovery of incriminating evidence.

title: Ranveer moves court in Don 3 row, sends legal notice to FWICE
content: Actor Ranveer Singh has legally challenged the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) after they issued a non-cooperation directive following his reported exit from 'Don 3'. Singh argues FWICE lacks jurisdiction over contractual matters. The dispute, which has caused significant financial implications for producers, has also seen a veteran producer filing a petition against FWICE's authority. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 191 attacks on healthcare, 128 dead: What WHO's latest Lebanon warning reveals
content: The World Health Organization raises serious concerns over the increasing violence targeting healthcare in Lebanon, where two hospitals in Tyre have suffered extensive damage, leaving many health professionals wounded. Since March 2026, the WHO has verified 191 attacks, signaling a humanitarian crisis.

title: Dad opposed Jio-to-Airtel port request: Why Bengaluru techie killed parents
content: A software engineer, Rohan Chandra, is accused of fatally stabbing his parents in Bengaluru. While initial reports suggested schizophrenia, doctors at Nimhans found him fit, citing financial disputes and a disagreement over a mobile number switch as the motive. Forensic evidence confirms Rohan's blood-stained clothes matched his parents'.

title: 'Tendulkar grew up in a different era': Manjrekar amid calls for Sooryavanshi's India debut
content: Young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's IPL heroics have ignited a debate on his national team prospects. While some advocate for a fast-track, former cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar urges caution, emphasizing the difference between T20 success and red-ball cricket. Manjrekar believes Sooryavanshi should chart his own career path, free from undue pressure and public speculation.

title: Norway Chess: Pragg humbles Carlsen once again; Gukesh, Divya suffer classical losses
content: At Norway Chess Round 8, R Praggnanandhaa defeated Magnus Carlsen for the second time in two weeks, outplaying the World No. 1 in a sharp French Defence to stay in title contention. Meanwhile, Gukesh suffered another classical setback against Alireza Firouzja after a preparation mix-up in the Ragozin Defence. In the women’s event, Divya Deshmukh lost to women's leader Bibisara Assaubayeva.

title: DKS to open CM stint with 8 populist schemes; Sidda appointed to CWC
content: DK Shivakumar is set to be sworn in as Karnataka Chief Minister this afternoon, immediately announcing eight populist schemes targeting farmers and women. This move aims to define his government's policy direction, drawing parallels with past welfare initiatives. The ceremony will be attended by top Congress leaders and will feature an open-jeep procession.

title: Month four of Hormuz blockade: Oil prices jump after Iran attacks Kuwait, Bahrain
content: Middle East tensions surged as Iran launched missiles towards Kuwait and Bahrain, prompting US retaliatory strikes. Despite ongoing peace talks, no breakthrough has occurred, and the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted for 96 days.  Meanwhile, US crude inventories saw a significant drop for the seventh consecutive week, adding to market volatility.

title: PM Modi, Trump likely to come face to face at G7 summit
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Donald Trump are expected to cross paths at the G7 summit in France, with both leaders confirmed to attend. While a bilateral meeting is being explored on the sidelines, no official engagement has been finalized yet.

title: SC sets up expert panel on Aravalis, petitioners question its independence
content: The Supreme Court has formed a five-member high-powered committee to redefine the 'Aravali hills and ranges', dropping the controversial 100m height criterion. This committee will assess the ecological impact of regulated mining and identify areas excluded from protection, aiming to prevent further degradation of the ancient mountain ranges.

title: Dhurandhar: Ranveer, Aditya took smaller fees after massive success — here's why
content: Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar franchise has rewritten the box-office record books, with Dhurandhar and Dhurandhar: Revenge together earning more than Rs 3,000 crore worldwide. But according to producer Jyoti Deshpande, the journey to creating one of Indian cinema's biggest franchises was far from straightforward. She revealed how the makers navigated ballooning budgets, reimagined the project as a two-part saga midway through production, and adopted a unique risk-sharing model involving lead star Ranveer Singh and director Aditya Dhar.

title: 'Our bus was going up in smoke': Jos Buttler reveals scary incident after GT's IPL final loss
content: Gujarat Titans' Jos Buttler described a frightening safety scare after their IPL final loss. The team bus caught fire due to a short circuit, forcing players and staff to evacuate.  This incident compounded their disappointment after falling short against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, adding a bizarre end to their campaign.

title: PM Modi to chair June 11 Niti governing council meet
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will lead a crucial Niti Aayog governing council meeting on June 11, following a recent restructuring. The agenda includes reviewing chief secretaries' deliberations and actions taken on reforms aimed at boosting manufacturing and self-reliance. This comes as the nation grapples with economic pressures from the West Asia conflict, impacting the rupee and raw material prices.

title: Married daughter entitled to compassionate job: SC
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that married daughters are entitled to be considered part of a 'family' for employment or ration shop allotment if their parents die in harness. The court found a UP government order excluding married daughters from the definition of 'family' unconstitutional, citing gender-based stereotypes and the principle of equality.

title: 'Disappointment weighs heavy': Gill rues missed opportunity after IPL final loss
content: In a message posted on Instagram, Gill reflected on the loss and thanked fans for their support throughout the season.

title: Patna: Shots fired outside Khan Sir's coaching centre; rivalry angle suspected
content: Shots were fired outside educator Khan Sir's coaching institute in Patna, injuring a security guard. The incident is suspected to be linked to rivalries between local coaching institute operators. The injured guard is receiving medical treatment, and authorities are gathering statements to further investigate the physical assault.

title: SIT reveals law enforcement’s efforts to shield former Kerala CM Pinarayi’s security detail from prosecution in Youth Congress attack case
content: In a statement of fact submitted at Alappuzha District and Sessions Court, charging officer M. Baiju Paulose claimed SIT came across instances of brazen subversion of law

title: 'Khan Sir' coaching institute in Patna vandalised, pelted with stones
content: Faisal Khan also claimed that firing took place at the institution. The police, however, denied the claim

title: Ramesh presses Centre over Great Nicobar clearances
content: Jairam Ramesh and Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav have exchanged several letters over the issue with the former slamming the government over the project and claiming it would cause great ecological damage

title: USTR proposes 12.5% additional duties on India, 53 other countries over forced labour import violations
content: The action follows investigations launched against 60 countries over what the USTR described as their failure to impose and effectively enforce bans on imports made with forced labour

title: AP Waqf Board chief urges Muthavallis to register on UMEED portal by June 6
content: Andhra Pradesh Waqf Board chairman Abdul Aziz asks Muthavallis who have not registered their properties to do so before the UMEED portal deadline on June 6

title: U.T. Khader quits as Assembly Speaker, set to become Minister
content: Khader, the MLA representing the Mangaluru constituency, submitted his resignation to Deputy Speaker Rudrappa Lamani.

title: Selvaperunthagai to visit Karunanidhi’s mausoleum; Congress eyes Rajya Sabha seat from Tamil Nadu
content: At a time when the Congress and DMK are no longer in alliance, Mr. Selvaperunthagai’s visit to the memorial has fuelled speculation; meanwhile, Girish Chodankar is expected to meet CM Vijay and seek an RS seat for Congress

title: As screens shrink, Tamil entertainment finds new ways to tell stories
content: KadhaiShorts, a micro drama platform launched recently, aims to bring vertical story telling packaged into snackable two-minute episodic content to the Tamil audience across the world

title: Congress bets on D.K. Shivakumar with eye on 2028 polls, Vokkaliga vote and Old Mysore region
content: The party wants to build a political combination by wooing a chunk of Vokkaliga voters, besides the traditional AHINDA voters, by elevating D.K. Shivakumar

title: B.K. Hariprasad likely to become KPCC president
content: Mr. Hariprasad is known as a vocal critic of the Narendra Modi-led government, the BJP, and the RSS. He has also served as a Rajya Sabha member and held several positions within the AICC.

title: Karnataka CM swearing-in LIVE: D.K. Shivakumar to be sworn in as Karnataka CM
content: Siddaramaiah resigned as CM last week, as directed by the Congress high command, paving the way for Shivakumar to take the coveted position

title: Siddaramaiah and D.K. Shivakumar: A public bonhomie that never let up
content: On May 28, when Mr. Siddaramaiah eventually resigned, Mr. Shivakumar was seen touching his feet and both were clicked in a warm embrace

title: Supreme Court forms high-powered expert panel to re-examine Aravalli definition
content: The committee will assess if the Aravalli hills over 100 metres high form a continuous ecological area despite exceeding the proposed 500-metre gap and whether mining should be allowed in those spaces

title: Annamalai quits BJP, to start new political ‘movement’
content: Sources told The Hindu on Wednesday that Mr. Annamalai submitted his resignation letter to the party leadership on Tuesday

title: Elevation of D.K. Shivakumar likely to heat up politics in the Vokkliaga belt
content: The Bidadi township project, initiated by Kumaraswamy but being pushed by Shivakumar currently, is expected to turn into flashpoint

title: Will join CJP protest on June 6 if Education Minister doesn’t resign: Sonam Wangchuk
content: Mr. Wangchuk cited broader concerns over the state of education and the implementation of reforms in the country as reasons behind his decision

title: Shivakumar’s elevation as Karnataka CM poses existential challenge for JD(S)
content: Party leaders weigh return of H.D. Kumaraswamy to State politics amid concerns over Congress consolidating Vokkaliga support ahead of 2028 Assembly elections

title: D.K. Shivakumar: Congress troubleshooter poised to become Chief Minister of Karnataka
content: D.K. Shivakumar’s proximity to the party’s top brass, particularly Sonia Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, is believed to have strengthened his claim after a prolonged power struggle with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah over the last three years

title: D.K. Shivakumar: A trusted troubleshooter's ascent to the top job in Karnataka
content: Known as one of Karnataka’s most resourceful Congress leaders as well as one of its wealthiest politicians, here's a look at D.K. Shivakumar's poltica

title: Seasoned diplomat Vipul appointed India's next ambassador to Saudi Arabia
content: Mr. Vipul, a 1998-batch Indian Foreign Service (IFS) officer, is currently serving as Indian ambassador to Qatar

title: Infections, fractures increasingly driving repeat hip replacement surgeries, finds study
content: With an increase in both young and elderly patients undergoing joint replacement surgery and their longer life span with implants, the overall number and complexity of revision surgeries are rising over time

title: After smooth transition, new Karnataka CM has a delicate task ahead
content: One of the biggest challenges before the new Chief Minister would be balancing his relationship with Siddaramaiah’s loyalists and support base within the Congress

title: BJP keen to counter an aggressive D.K. Shivakumar in his own style
content: The saffron party is also hoping that there will be issues of conflict between supporters of Siddaramaiah and DKS that they can leverage

title: Kerala Assembly: LDF accuses UDF of unfair mass transfers of government staff, stages walkout
content: Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan says government transferred around 2,000 employees in violation of transfer rules. UDF refutes charges

title: Maharashtra government acquires Mumbai’s iconic Air India building in ₹1,601 crore deal
content: The Air India building, a 23-storey sea-facing commercial tower at Nariman Point in south Mumbai built in 1974 on State government-owned reclaimed land, has long been one of the city’s landmarks

title: Karnataka CM swearing-in ceremony: Elaborate security in place at Lok Bhavan in Bengaluru
content: Given the limited seating capacity at Glass House in Lok Bhavan, entry has been restricted strictly to invited guests and passes have already been issued to VIPs, VVIPs and other categorised invitees.

title: D.K. Shivakumar thanks Gandhi family for his political rise
content: On X, Mr. Shivakumar said, “The Gandhi family has played a defining role in my political journey, not merely through leadership but through the faith they placed in me at different stages of my life.”

title: Strict entry guidelines issued for CM’s oath-taking ceremony at Lok Bhavan in Bengaluru
content: The advisory issued by authorities states that only individuals holding valid invitation passes will be allowed entry into Lok Bhavan

title: The Huddle to look at Aravallis, Nicobar, and India’s development debate
content: Former Environment Minister Jairam Ramesh and conservation biologist Krithi Karanth to take part in session on June 6

title: The Hindu Huddle on Campus to be held at Amity University in Bengaluru on June 3
content: The Hindu Huddle on Campus is an initiative to create spaces for dialogue, debate, and critical thinking among the youth

title: Telangana CM to share views on governance and welfare delivery at The Hindu Huddle
content: The session will cover Telangana’s Hyderabad-led economic development over the past decade, with the double-digit annual growth rate ranking well above the national average

title: The Hindu Huddle panel to throw light on AI and its impact
content: Secretary, MeitY, S. Krishnan; Chief Economic Advisor V. Anantha Nageswaran; and former CEO, Cognizant Lakshmi Narayanan will discuss how India should prepare for the emerging threat to employment

title: Stage set for D.K. Shivakumar to take over as new CM of Karnataka
content: About 10 to 15 Ministers likely to be sworn in on Wednesday; no clarity on Deputy Chief Minister posts

title: Karnataka launches antimicrobial resistance surveillance programme in collaboration with TIGS
content: It is aimed at identifying resistance trends, emerging microbial threats and geographical variations in antimicrobial resistance

title: Searches carried out in six Kashmir districts in terror related case
content: The case registered in 2015 pertains to Pakistan-sponsored terrorist outfits, their sleeper-cell networks and activities related to recruitment, radicalisation and facilitating terrorism in J&K; searches were conducted at in Srinagar, Bandipora, Kupwara, Anantnag, Kulgam and Baramulla districts

title: D.K. Shivakumar | ‘Rock’ of Congress
content: The party’s crisis manager, known for his organisational skills and grassroots networks, is set to become Karnataka’s Chief Minister

title: Former Karnataka MLC kidnapped, released after extortion bid at Attibele in Bengaluru
content: The alleged incident occurred around 12.15 a.m. on June 2 when Mr. Reddy was returning home with his driver after finishing work at the Gold Coin Club, of which he is the president

title: Second and third year convocation held at Nrupathunga Cluster University
content: Karnataka Film Chamber of Commerce president Jayamala Ramachandra, senior litterateur S.G. Siddaramaiah and others, were awarded honorary doctorates on the occasion of convocation ceremony of Nrupathunga Cluster University on Tuesday

title: INDIA bloc to meet on June 8 amid strain; Trinamool, DMK set to attend
content: 17 Opposition parties likely to join the meeting; all eyes on DMK, given the recent tensions with Congress for switching sides in Tamil Nadu

title: ₹36,585 crore farm loan waiver to benefit 56 lakh farmers in Maharashtra
content: The scheme will cover more than 65 lakh accounts, and loans worth ₹36,585 crore of nearly 56 lakh farmers are expected to be waived

title: Tribal leaders, PDP demand release of arrested nomadic community leader Talib Hussain in Jammu
content: PDP leader Iltija Mufti, who condemned the arrest of Mr. Hussain, said, “The arrest of a political leader for peacefully voicing the concerns of vulnerable and marginalised citizens reflects an alarming intolerance towards dissent and democratic expression

title: Sarthak Sidhant flags CBSE OSM tender irregularities before Parliamentary panel
content: 17-year-old from Jharkhand presents findings before Standing Committee on Education as CBSE defends process, promises fixes

title: After a gap of 21 years, M.P. government to relaunch State-wide public bus service
content: The bus service ‘Mukhya Mantri Sugam Parivahan Sewa’, Mr. Yadav said, would be launched in phases across Madhya Pradesh with the government planning to start the services before Rakshabandhan.

title: PRISM initiative revolutionises public education in Kozhikode
content: Former Kozhikode North legislator A. Pradeepkumar, who spearheaded the project, said, “We wanted students to become winners not only in examinations but in life as well.”

title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah resigns, but won’t budge from State politics
content: Going down memory lane, he said he was an ‘accidental politician’, with none in the family being in politics

title: Nepal has also encroached upon Indian territory, says PM Balendra Shah
content: Lawmakers from the Nepali Congress and the Nepal Communist Party demanded that the Prime Minister clarify where exactly Nepal has encroached upon Indian territory and sought the removal of his remarks from the record.

title: No place for third parties in border issues: MEA in response to Nepal PM while hosting country’s ruling party chief
content: MEA spokesperson responds to claims made by Balen Shah who had asserted that the boundary issue is a complex one with both India and Nepal having possession of each other’s territory and had sought intervention of the U.K. and China

title: Sulur sexual assault-cum-murder case: witnesses identify main accused
content: Two eyewitnesses in the case, who claimed to have seen Karthi taking away the minor girl in a two-wheeler on May 21 evening, identified him from the line-up comprising other individuals, said sources

title: DRDO, IAF successfully flight-test indigenous RudraM-II air-to-surface missile
content: This marks a significant milestone in India’s efforts to strengthen its precision strike capabilities and advance self-reliance in defence technology

title: Tigress Zeenat gives birth to four cubs in Similipal
content: Forest Department takes special measures to ensure safety of mother and cubs, and their movements are being continuously monitored, says Odisha CM Mohan Majhi

title: 'Replacing Bad Habit With Another': Ladakh's New Liquor Policy Sparks Row
content: Ladakh's new liquor policy, the administration said, aims at curbing narcotics and giving people a wider choice of low-alcoholic-content liquor in the region.

title: US Wants To End Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Amid Iran War: Its Impact On India
content: Data from energy intelligence firm Kpler shows India imported a record-shattering 2.3 million barrels per day of Russian crude last month, supported by the continued import of cargoes under the sanctions waiver.

title: 8th Pay Commission Delayed Again: What It Means For Salary, Pension Now
content: 8th Pay Commission: The original deadline was April 30, which was later extended to May 31. The latest order pushes the cut-off further to June 15.

title: Video: Termite-Infested Cash, 'Secret Room' Found In Kolkata College
content: The cash, comprising mostly Rs 100 and Rs 500 notes, was found during a cleanliness drive at Surendranath College ahead of the monsoon.

title: Kerala Toddler's Post-Mortem Reveals 7 Fractured Ribs, Burns
content: Doctors recorded 91 injuries on the child's body, including seven fractured ribs, internal injuries, and swelling and bleeding inside the skull.

title: K Annamalai's 200-Day Foot March That Finetuned BJP's Tamil Nadu Blueprint
content: There is a buzz that K Annamalai's departure is meant to make way for the launch of his new party. The 41-year-old already runs a non-profit leadership initiative called "We The Leaders", which may morph into his political project.

title: "Lucky You're In Kerala": Court To Kumbh Fame Monalisa, Husband Over 'Threat'
content: Kochi, June 2 (IANS) The Kerala High Court on Tuesday reserved its verdict on the anticipatory bail plea filed by Kumbh Mela social media sensation Monalisa Bhosle and her husband, Mohammed Farmaan Khan, in a case that has evolved from an interfaith

title: 'Singam' K Annamalai: BJP's Tamil Nadu Star Rose Fast, Fell Faster
content: K Annamalai flew down to Delhi, met party chief Nitin Nabin on Tuesday and said he wants to end the partnership on "cordial terms".

title: Maharashtra Government Acquires Iconic Air India Building For Rs 1,601 Crore
content: Maharashtra government acquires Air India building in south Mumbai for Rs 1,601 crore to consolidate offices.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 550 Points Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: US mulls ending Russian oil sanctions waiver. Decision to benefit India.

title: Drug Smugglers Throw Currency In 'Farzi'-Style From Car To Evade Arrest In UP
content: The incident took place earlier this week, when a Honda City car ignored stop signals and sped away, prompting Special Operations Group (SOG) personnel to give chase.

title: Elon Musk Could Become World's First Trillionaire With SpaceX's Market Debut
content: The valuation of the SpaceX, founded in 2002, is expected to range from $1.7 trillion to $2 trillion, up from a range that topped out at $1.5 trillion in March.

title: Trump Orders AI Developers To Share Models With Government Before Release
content: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order creating a voluntary framework under which AI developers will share advanced models with...

title: 2 Trinamool Councillors Arrested For Extortion Amid BJP's Action On Corruption
content: Sachin Singh and Arijit Das Thakur - two Trinamool councillors arrested on Tuesday - face extortion charges.

title: Video shows moment Hellfire missile cripples tanker bound for Iran's Kharg Island
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: Turmoil in Trinamool: How the 'fake signature' row challenges Mamata's grip on party
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: Over 550 Indian peacekeepers in South Sudan awarded UN Medal of Honour
content: at least 565 peacekeepers, including 53 women, have been honoured . blue Helmets refers to military personnel, police officers and civilian experts . india is the second largest contributor of uniformed personnel to UN peacekeeping operations .

title: Hormuz shut, shipping services through other routes to West Asia up 2x
content: the number of shipping services operating east of Hormuz and through the Red Sea rose from 127 in febr to 257 in April and 245 in may . there has been a significant increase in services east and in the red sea, underscoring the resilience of Indian maritime trade and sustained confidence among shipping stakeholders .
